content:
Hi,

I'm interested in the current status of the Rust net_device abstraction for network device drivers.

From what I can find:

  • The staging/rust-net branch in Rust-for-Linux/linux appears to be archived/unmaintained (listed under "Past branches" on rust-for-linux.com).
  • FUJITA Tomonori's [PATCH v2] Rust abstractions for network device drivers (Jul 2023, rust/kernel/net/dev.rs) seems to have stalled after v2.
  • The PHY driver abstraction (phylib) was merged into mainline, but the net_device / net_device_ops abstraction has not.

My questions:

  1. Is there a more recent branch or WIP effort for the net_device abstraction that I may have missed?
  2. Is anyone currently working on or planning to revive this effort?

Any pointers would be appreciated. Thanks!

I would suggest searching in lore.kernel.org (e.g. please see my message at https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/CANiq72kRoHsLx7GSrRq-OP-mz6Y9nru8UMp4dcvdmNJasGZcuw@mail.gmail.com/ from yesterday), plus asking in #Networking, plus sending an email to the relevant maintainers.

I hope that helps! Cc'ing @FUJITA Tomonori and @Trevor Gross here in case it helps too.

Thank you for the detailed and kind response, Miguel!

For context, my immediate goal is a personal project — I'm looking to rebase the net_device abstraction onto v7.0-rc1 for my own use (cherry-picking what I need). I don't have plans to submit patches upstream at this point, just exploring for a personal use case. So I'll dig deeper into lore.kernel.org for the existing netdevice abstraction patches and related discussions.

Your suggestions on finding areas to contribute are very helpful — I'll keep them in mind if I decide to get more involved. Thanks again!
Could an admin/moderator move this topic to #Networking? I don't seem to have the option to do so. Thanks!

#Networking > ✔ `SkBuff`
I think this topic is the answer
This topic was moved here from #Help > net_device abstraction / staging/rust-net status by Miguel Ojeda.

Could an admin/moderator move this topic to #Networking? I don't seem to have the option to do so. Thanks!

Done!

Thank you for the detailed and kind response, Miguel!

You're welcome :)
